```fortran
module my_module
contains
    subroutine cb(x)
    implicit none
    integer, intent(in), optional :: x(:)
    end subroutine cb
end module my_module

program test
    use my_module
    call temp(cb)

contains
    subroutine temp(call_back)
        implicit none
        procedure(cb) :: call_back
        logical :: terminate_var
        call call_back()   
    end subroutine temp
end program test
```

program intrinsics_57
implicit none

character(len=:), allocatable :: shorts(:)
character(len=:), allocatable :: long
integer :: a(10), i, res(10)
do i = 1, 10
    a(i) = i
end do

allocate(character(len=6) :: shorts(10))
allocate(character(len=5) :: long)
shorts(1:10:2) = "shorts"
shorts(2:10:2) = "long"
long = "long"

res = merge(1, a, shorts == long)
end program

```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2515223757/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

MRE :

module procedure_07_module

    subroutine cb(x)
        implicit none
        integer, intent(in), optional :: x(:)
    end subroutine cb

    subroutine calfun(x)
        implicit none
        integer, intent(in), optional :: x(:)
        logical :: y
        y = present(x)
        if (y) error stop
    end subroutine calfun

program procedure_07
    use procedure_07_module
    call temp(calfun)

    subroutine temp(call_back)
        implicit none
        procedure(cb) :: call_back
        call call_back()   
    end subroutine temp
end program procedure_07

is just one step ahead of #5500 

MRE : 
```fortran
module procedure_07_module
contains
    subroutine cb(x)
        implicit none
        integer, intent(in), optional :: x(:)
    end subroutine cb

    subroutine calfun(x)
        implicit none
        integer, intent(in), optional :: x(:)
        logical :: y
        y = present(x)
        if (y) error stop
    end subroutine calfun
end module procedure_07_module

program procedure_07
    use procedure_07_module
    call temp(calfun)
contains
    subroutine temp(call_back)
        implicit none
        procedure(cb) :: call_back
        call call_back()   
    end subroutine temp
end program procedure_07
```

MRE : 
```fortran
module procedure_07_module
contains
    subroutine cb(x)
        implicit none
        integer, intent(in), optional :: x(:)
    end subroutine cb

    subroutine calfun(x)
        implicit none
        integer, intent(in), optional :: x(:)
        logical :: y
        y = present(x)
        if (y) error stop
    end subroutine calfun
end module procedure_07_module

program procedure_07
    use procedure_07_module
    call temp(calfun)
contains
    subroutine temp(call_back)
        implicit none
        procedure(cb) :: call_back
        call call_back()   
    end subroutine temp
end program procedure_07
```

end program procedure_07

**File 1 :**
```fortran
module procedure_07_module
contains
    subroutine cb(x, a, b, terminate)
        implicit none
        real, intent(in) :: x(:)
        integer, intent(in), optional :: a
        integer, intent(in), optional :: b
        logical, intent(out), optional :: terminate
    end subroutine cb
end module procedure_07_module

module test
contains 
subroutine temp(call_back)
    use procedure_07_module
    implicit none
    procedure(cb) :: call_back
    logical :: terminate_var
    real :: x3(2)
    call call_back(x3, terminate = terminate_var)   
end subroutine temp
end module
```

**File 2 :**
```fortran
program procedure_07
    use procedure_07_module
    use test
    call temp(cb)
end program procedure_07
```

        implicit none

Minimal Reproducible Example

```fortran
module main_module

contains

   subroutine sub(int)
      integer,intent(in) :: int
      print *, int
      if (int /= 6) error stop
   end subroutine sub

end module main_module


program main

   use main_module

   procedure(sub), pointer :: p_sub
   p_sub => sub
   call p_sub(6)

end program main
```

### Output

```console
(lf) saurabh-kumar@Awadh:~/Projects/System/lfortran$ conda activate lf_release
(lf_release) saurabh-kumar@Awadh:~/Projects/System/lfortran$ lfortran test.f90
6
(lf_release) saurabh-kumar@Awadh:~/Projects/System/lfortran$ conda deactivate
(lf) saurabh-kumar@Awadh:~/Projects/System/lfortran$ lfortran test.f90
code generation error: Subroutine code not generated for 'sub'


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.

Minimal Reproducible Example

```fortran
program main

   procedure(say_something), pointer :: proc
   proc => say_something

   call proc("Hello from proc!")

contains

   subroutine say_something(message)
      character(len=*),intent(in) :: message
      print *, message
   end subroutine

end program main
```

code"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-12-20T13:56:25Z","updated_at":"2024-12-20T16:43:00Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"We need to handle this for language elements that support declarations. Please see `AST.asdl` for nodes with `unit_decl2*`. `AST::Module_t` can be handled in the same way as #5770. 

For others however, like `AST::Subroutine_t`, `AST::Function_t` we probably would need to extend the logic or do something different, as these can have self-referencing procedures. We have an issue open for this at #5735. Another case is `AST::DerivedType_t`, where we declare the procedure in the scope of the derived type, but have the subroutine/function declared outside.

{"action":"opened","number":5812,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/pulls/5812","id":2251149306,"node_id":"PR_kwDOCpMuHc6GLc_6","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5812","diff_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5812.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5812.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5812","number":5812,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"feat: Support ArrayConstant in Multidimensional ArrayItem Assignment ","user":{"login":"jinangshah21","id":165902846,"node_id":"U_kgDOCeN5_g","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/165902846?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21","html_url":"https://github.com/jinangshah21","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"With this if both side of assignment is `ArrayItem` with vector indices then all cases are covered. 
Assignment like `A([1,2]) = [5,5]` is yet to be done for multidimensional ArrayReference. 

Transforms : 
```fortran
A([1,2],[1,2]) = A([2,1],[2,1])
```
To : 
```fortran
do __2_t1 = lbound(a1, 2), ubound(a1, 2)
    do __1_t4 = lbound(a1, 1), ubound(a1, 1)
        temp_a1(__1_t4, __2_t1) = a1(__1_t4, __2_t1)
    end do
end do
do __2_t2 = lbound(temp1, 1), ubound(temp1, 1)
    do __1_t5 = lbound(temp2, 1), ubound(temp2,&
         1)
        a1(temp3(__1_t5), temp4(__2_t2)) = temp_a1(temp2(__1_t5), temp1(__2_t2))
    end do
end do
```

this if both side of assignment is `ArrayItem` with vector indices then all cases are covered. 
Assignment like `A([1,2]) = [5,5]` is yet to be done for multidimensional ArrayReference. 

Transforms : 
```fortran
A([1,2],[1,2]) = A([2,1],[2,1])
```
To : 
```fortran
do __2_t1 = lbound(a1, 2), ubound(a1, 2)
    do __1_t4 = lbound(a1, 1), ubound(a1, 1)
        temp_a1(__1_t4, __2_t1) = a1(__1_t4, __2_t1)
    end do
end do
do __2_t2 = lbound(temp1, 1), ubound(temp1, 1)
    do __1_t5 = lbound(temp2, 1), ubound(temp2,&
         1)
        a1(temp3(__1_t5), temp4(__2_t2)) = temp_a1(temp2(__1_t5), temp1(__2_t2))
    end do
end do
```

program test1
    integer :: B(4) = [1,2,3,4]
    B([2,1,3,4]) = B
    print *, B

Test 2: 
```fortran
program test2
    integer :: A(2,2) = reshape([1,2,3,4],[2,2])
    A(:, :) = A(:, [1,2])
    print *, A
end program
```

Test 3: (BinOp) 
```fortran
program test3
    integer :: A(2,2) = reshape([1,2,3,4],[2,2])
    integer :: A2(2,2) = reshape([4,5,6,7],[2,2])
    A([2,1],[2,1]) = A - A2
    print *, A
end program
```

Test 4: (Reshape)
```fortran
program test4
    integer :: A(2,2) = reshape([1,2,3,4],[2,2])
    integer :: B(4) = [1,2,3,4]
    A([2,1],[2,1]) = reshape(B, [2,2])
    print *, A
end program
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5849/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5849/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

```fortran
program test1
    integer :: B(4) = [1,2,3,4]
    B([2,1,3,4]) = B
    print *, B
end program
```

Test 2: 
```fortran
program test2
    integer :: A(2,2) = reshape([1,2,3,4],[2,2])
    A(:, :) = A(:, [1,2])
    print *, A
end program
```

Test 3: (BinOp) 
```fortran
program test3
    integer :: A(2,2) = reshape([1,2,3,4],[2,2])
    integer :: A2(2,2) = reshape([4,5,6,7],[2,2])
    A([2,1],[2,1]) = A - A2
    print *, A
end program
```

Test 4: (Reshape)
```fortran
program test4
    integer :: A(2,2) = reshape([1,2,3,4],[2,2])
    integer :: B(4) = [1,2,3,4]
    A([2,1],[2,1]) = reshape(B, [2,2])
    print *, A
end program
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5849/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5849/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2563847520","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5849#issuecomment-2563847520","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5849","id":2563847520,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6Y0TVg","user":{"login":"jinangshah21","id":165902846,"node_id":"U_kgDOCeN5_g","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/165902846?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21","html_url":"https://github.com/jinangshah21","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-27T16:22:34Z","updated_at":"2024-12-27T16:22:34Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Simplifer pass handles it but just we have to create temporary for RHS wherever LHS and RHS has same variables.

```fortran 
program procedure_08
    call temp(calfun)
contains

    subroutine calfun(x) !! Actual function to be called
        implicit none
        integer, intent(inout) :: x(:)
        x = 2
    end subroutine calfun

    subroutine cb(x) !! Called function
        implicit none
        integer, intent(inout) :: x(:)
        x = 1
    end subroutine cb

    subroutine temp(call_back)
        implicit none
        procedure(cb) :: call_back
        call temp2(call_back)
    end subroutine temp

    subroutine temp2(call_back)
        implicit none
        procedure(cb) :: call_back
        integer :: x(4) 
        call call_back(x) 
        print *, x
        if(x(1) /= 2) error stop
    end subroutine temp2
end program procedure_08
```

Here `call call_back(x)` in `temp2` should call `calfun` instead of `cb`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5868/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5868/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

function"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-12-29T19:09:04Z","updated_at":"2024-12-30T08:25:06Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"MRE : 
```fortran
program test
    character(len=:), allocatable :: string 
    character(len=*), parameter :: newline = new_line('')
    integer :: j
    string = "hello" // new_line('') // "world"
    j = index(string, newline) 
    print *, j
end program 
```

Here j should be 6 but output is 1. 

Also if we do `j = index(string, new_line(' '))`  then it works so I think runtime arg should be handled.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5869/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5869/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2565163547","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5869#issuecomment-2565163547","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5869","id":2565163547,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6Y5Uob","user":{"login":"jinangshah21","id":165902846,"node_id":"U_kgDOCeN5_g","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/165902846?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21","html_url":"https://github.com/jinangshah21","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-30T08:25:05Z","updated_at":"2024-12-30T08:25:05Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Oh I just found issue is not in `index` intrinsic But issue is when we pass newline as argument in function where newline is declared as parameter. 

So actual MRE : 
```fortran
program test
    character(len=*), parameter :: newline = new_line('')
    call temp(newline)

contains 
   subroutine temp(str)
      character(len=*) :: str
      print *, len(str)
    end subroutine
end program 
```

Also if we use `character(len=:), allocatable :: newline` then it works.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2565163547/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

```fortran
module inf_mod_1
implicit none
private
public::is_finite
interface is_finite
    module procedure is_finite_1
end interface is_finite
contains
subroutine is_finite_1() 
end subroutine is_finite_1
end module inf_mod_1

module infnan_mod_1
use, non_intrinsic :: inf_mod_1, only : is_finite
end module infnan_mod_1

program bobyqa_exmp
contains
subroutine calfun(x)
use, non_intrinsic :: infnan_mod_1   !!! Here we don't use only
real, intent(in) :: x(:)
end subroutine calfun
end program bobyqa_exmp
```

It is working by just removing `only`  in calfun from MRE. And then ASR is as below : 

```fortran
is_finite:
            (ExternalSymbol
            6
            is_finite
            2 is_finite
            inf_mod_1
            []
            is_finite
            Public
            ),
            is_finite_1:
            (ExternalSymbol
            6
            is_finite_1
            2 is_finite_1
            inf_mod_1
            []
            is_finite_1
            Public
            ),
```
So it is storing `is_finite` as ExternalSymbol only.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2469615174/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

module inf_mod_1
interface is_finite
    module procedure is_finite_1
end interface is_finite
contains
subroutine is_finite_1() 
end subroutine is_finite_1
end module inf_mod_1

module infnan_mod_1
use, non_intrinsic :: inf_mod_1, only : is_finite
contains
subroutine is_finite_1(x)
integer , intent(in) :: x
print *, x 
end subroutine is_finite_1
end module infnan_mod_1


program bobyqa_exmp
use, non_intrinsic :: infnan_mod_1
call is_finite_1(1)
end program bobyqa_exmp
```

LFortran Error :
```console
semantic error: More actual than formal arguments in procedure call
  --> integration_tests/a.f90:22:18
   |
22 | call is_finite_1(1)
   |                  ^ 
```

Here issue is in SymbolTable of program `bobyqa_exmp` where `is_finte_1` is stored as ExternalSymbol for is_finite_1 of inf_mod_1 and we have no information of is_finite_1 of infnan_mod_1. Can you post an ASR before and after this PR, just for the expression involved? That way we can check that the new way is correct.

Previously it was not generating ASR. Basically we have to do `size(abs(A(:,1:x))` so in `make_ArraySize_util_t` size will get `IntrinsicElementalFunction` as argument and inside it we have `ArraySection`. What `make_ArraySize_util_t` does is it checks of argument is ArraySection first then it checks for `IntrinsicElementalFunction`. From `IntrinsicElementalFunction` check we will get ArraySection which we have to process further but ArraySection check is done before so it gives : 
```
function make_ArraySize_t_util(), line number 2637 at
ASR::is_a<ASR::Var_t>(*a_v) || ASR::is_a<ASR::StructInstanceMember_t>(*a_v) || ASR::is_a<ASR::FunctionParam_t>(*a_v)
ERROR MESSAGE:
Found ASR::exprType::67  !!ArraySection
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2470961147/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

```fortran
program test 
    implicit none
    real(8) :: y(2,2)
    real(8) :: x(2) = [1,2]
    real(8) :: z(size(y,2))
    integer :: j = 1
    z = matprod12(x, y)
    print *, z

contains
function matprod12(x, y) result(z)
    implicit none
    real(8), intent(in) :: x(:)
    real(8), intent(in) :: y(:, :)
    real(8) :: z(size(y, 2))
    integer :: j 
    j = size(y,2)
    z(j) = inprod(x, y(:, j))
end function matprod12

function inprod(x, y) result(z)
    implicit none
    real(8), intent(in) :: x(:)
    real(8), intent(in) :: y(:)
    real(8) :: z 
end function inprod
end program

cmake --build build --target install

working"},{"id":6685198782,"node_id":"LA_kwDOCpMuHc8AAAABjngJvg","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/labels/intrinsic","name":"intrinsic","color":"53BBFE","default":false,"description":"Issue function"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-09-15T12:19:49Z","updated_at":"2024-10-06T18:14:07Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"## Minimal reproducible example

```f90

PROGRAM test_spread
  INTEGER :: a = 1
  WRITE(*,*) SPREAD(A, 1, 2)
END PROGRAM
```

the above fortran program, taken from https://gcc.gnu.org/onlinedocs/gfortran/SPREAD.html. On compilation with LFortran raises the below error:
```bash
semantic error: The argument `source` in `spread` must be of type Array
   --> test.f90:280:23
    |
280 |     WRITE(*,*) SPREAD(A, 1, 2)
    |                       ^ 


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
```

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/pulls/comments/1794055325","pull_request_review_id":2358023225,"id":1794055325,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOCpMuHc5q7xyd","diff_hunk":"@@ -3236,18 +3236,29 @@ namespace FindLoc {
         int dim = 0, kind = 4; // default kind
         ASR::expr_t *dim_expr = nullptr;
         ASR::expr_t *mask_expr = nullptr;
-        if (args[2]) {
-            dim_expr = args[2];
-            if ( !ASR::is_a<ASR::Integer_t>(*expr_type(args[2])) ) {
-                append_error(diag, "`dim` should be a scalar integer type", loc);
+        
+        // Checking for type findLoc(Array, value, mask)
+        if( args[2] && !args[3] && is_logical(*expr_type(args[2])) ){","path":"src/libasr/pass/intrinsic_array_function_registry.h","commit_id":"f0cb47b340b6b32605173b07596e913189b37232","original_commit_id":"f0cb47b340b6b32605173b07596e913189b37232","user":{"login":"jinangshah21","id":165902846,"node_id":"U_kgDOCeN5_g","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/165902846?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21","html_url":"https://github.com/jinangshah21","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"> The right design in my opinion is to use the "overload_id" in ASR for this. We discussed exactly this issue for a similar function, I can't remember which one. You decide on the signatures that you will support, let's just say the following:
> 
> ```
> minloc(array) ! overload_id = 0
> minloc(array, dim) ! overload_id = 1
> minloc(array, dim, mask) ! overload_id = 2
> ```
> 
> And for each case (`overload_id`) all arguments are required and never null (it could be that some arguments have to have their default values specified). So you never write code like this:
> 
> ```
>  if( args[2] && !args[3] && is_logical(*expr_type(args[2])) 
> ```
> 
> Rather, you write code like `if (overload_id == 2) { /* array, dim, mask is present and valid, handle this case */ }`.
> 
> The general design of ASR is that we do not have defaults or optional arguments. Everything is always present and explicit. So there is no branching logic and trying to figure out the defaults, like you discovered in this PR. All these decisions are made by the frontend. Once ASR is constructed, it is already set it stone what exact operations we need to do.
> 
> Let me know if you have any questions about this and if you know how to apply this to this PR, or want to discuss more.

By Checking `if( args[2] && !args[3] && is_logical(*expr_type(args[2])) ` I am differentiating between overload_id for (Array, value, dim)(i.e. 1) and (Array, value, mask)(i.e. 2). So it uses overload_id MRE : 
```fortran
program linalg_mod
implicit none
real :: B(2,2) = reshape([2,3,4,5],[2,2])
integer :: x(2) = [2,1]
B = B(:,x)
print *, B
end program
```

LFortran Error : 
```diff
(lf) jinu2110@JNSLAP:/mnt/c/lfortran$ lfortran a.f90 
semantic error: Incompatible ranks 2 and 1 in assignment
 --> a.f90:5:1
  |
5 | B = B(:,x)
  | ^^^^^^^^^^ 
```

GFortran Output : 
```
(lf) jinu2110@JNSLAP:/mnt/c/lfortran$ gfortran a.f90 && ./a.out
   4.00000000       5.00000000       2.00000000       3.00000000  
```

MRE : 
```fortran
program linalg_mod
implicit none
real :: B(2,2) = reshape([2,3,4,5],[2,2])
integer :: x(2) = [2,1]
B = B(:,x)
print *, B
end program
```

LFortran Error : 
```
(lf) jinu2110@JNSLAP:/mnt/c/lfortran$ lfortran a.f90 
semantic error: Incompatible ranks 2 and 1 in assignment
 --> a.f90:5:1
  |
5 | B = B(:,x)
  | ^^^^^^^^^^ 
```

GFortran Output : 
```
(lf) jinu2110@JNSLAP:/mnt/c/lfortran$ gfortran a.f90 && ./a.out
   4.00000000       5.00000000       2.00000000       3.00000000  
```

```console

```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4397/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4397/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2430097119","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4397#issuecomment-2430097119","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4397","id":2430097119,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6Q2Fbf","user":{"login":"jinangshah21","id":165902846,"node_id":"U_kgDOCeN5_g","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/165902846?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21","html_url":"https://github.com/jinangshah21","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jinangshah21/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-22T19:37:26Z","updated_at":"2024-10-22T19:37:26Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> To compile PRIMA with GFortran :
> 
> ```
> git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/shivimathur/prima
> cd prima 
> git checkout -t origin/lf
> cmake -S . -B build -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=install -DCMAKE_Fortran_COMPILER=gfortran
> cmake --build build --target install
> ```

In Compilation of PRIMA, just before cmake do : 
1) Add "--cpp" flag in `CMakeLists.txt` of Prima as follows : `set(CMAKE_Fortran_FLAGS "${CMAKE_Fortran_FLAGS} --cpp")`
2) Run : 
```
cmake -S . -B build -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=install -DCMAKE_Fortran_COMPILER=lfortran -DCMAKE_Fortran_FLAGS="--cpp"
cmake --build build --target install
```

That will take care of preprocessor Directives.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2430097119/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Fortran Code : 
```fortran
module my_module
contains
    subroutine cb(a,terminate)
        implicit none
        integer, intent(in), optional :: a
        logical, intent(out), optional :: terminate
    end subroutine cb
end module my_module

program test
    use my_module
    logical :: terminate_var
    call temp(cb)

contains

    subroutine temp(call_back)
        implicit none
        procedure(cb) :: call_back
        logical :: terminate_var
        call call_back(terminate = terminate_var)   
    end subroutine temp

end program test
```

LFortran Error : 
```
semantic error: Keyword arguments are not implemented for generic subroutines yet, symbol type: 9
  --> b.f90:21:9
   |
21 |         call call_back(terminate = terminate_var)
   |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

It works with GFortran.

**Some things I noticed with LFortran**: 
1) If we keep both code in one file it works.
2) If we remove Procedure(OBJCON) then it works. 
3) If I keep OBJCON subroutine in evalute_mod module itself then it works.

It works with GFortran.

**Some things I noticed with LFortran**: 
1) If we keep both code in one file it works.
2) If we remove Procedure(OBJCON) in evaluate_mod module then it works. 
3) If I keep OBJCON subroutine in evaluate_mod module itself then it works.

**file 1** : 
```fortran
module temp
contains
    subroutine OBJCON()
    end subroutine OBJCON
end module temp

module evaluate_mod
contains
subroutine evaluate(calcfc)
    use temp
    implicit none
    procedure(OBJCON) :: calcfc 
end subroutine evaluate
end module evaluate_mod
```
**file 2** : 
```fortran
program initialize
use, non_intrinsic :: evaluate_mod
end program initialize
```

```

It works with GFortran.

**Some things I noticed with LFortran**: 
1) If we keep both code in one file it works.
2) If we remove Procedure(OBJCON) in evaluate_mod module then it works. 
3) If I keep OBJCON subroutine in evaluate_mod module itself then it works.

**file 1** : 
```fortran
module temp
contains
    subroutine OBJCON()
    end subroutine OBJCON
end module temp

module evaluate_mod
contains
subroutine evaluate(calcfc)
    use temp
    implicit none
    procedure(OBJCON) :: calcfc 
end subroutine evaluate
end module evaluate_mod
```
**file 2** : 
```fortran
program initialize
use, non_intrinsic :: evaluate_mod
end program initialize
```

It works with GFortran.

**Some things I noticed with LFortran**: 
1) If we keep both code in one file it works.
2) If we remove Procedure(OBJCON) in evaluate_mod module then it works. 
3) If I keep OBJCON subroutine in evaluate_mod module itself then it works.

It is needed because when we will call calcfc it will visit SubroutineCall :  
```fortran 
module eval_dependency
contains
    subroutine OBJCON()
    end subroutine OBJCON
end module eval_dependency

module procedure_decl_01_b
contains
subroutine evaluate(calcfc)
    use eval_dependency
    implicit none
    procedure(OBJCON) :: calcfc 
    **call calcfc()**
end subroutine evaluate
end module procedure_decl_01_b
```

If I am specifying ```EXTERNAL f```` in body of ```HINIT853(f)``` , code doesn't throw error.

In Gfortran : 

```PROGRAM test_present
  EXTERNAL func
  print *, f(func)
END PROGRAM

FUNCTION f(func)
    f = func(3)

real FUNCTION func(x)
    real::x
    f = x
  END FUNCTION```

is valid still we haven't declared func in f(). So I think we should exclude case when argument is a function and we are returning that funtion?

```
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

define float @f(i32* %x) {
.entry:
  %f = alloca float, align 4
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  %0 = load float, float* %f, align 4
  ret float %0
}

define double @hinit853(double* %f) {
.entry:
  %hinit853 = alloca double, align 8
  br label %return

unreachable_after_return:                         ; No predecessors!
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %unreachable_after_return, %.entry
  %0 = load double, double* %hinit853, align 8
  ret double %0
}

define void @sub(double ()* %f) {
.entry:
  %h = alloca double, align 8
  %0 = call double @hinit853(double ()* %f)
  store double %0, double* %h, align 8
  br label %return

unreachable_after_return:                         ; No predecessors!
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %unreachable_after_return, %.entry
  ret void
}

declare double @f.1()

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  call void @sub(float (i32*)* @f)
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)
asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
Call parameter type does not match function signature!
double ()* %f
 double*  %0 = call double @hinit853(double ()* %f)

float (i32*)* @f
 double ()*  call void @sub(float (i32*)* @f)
